scope_note: QA Polyphase Sum Structure cert [304]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.X; Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci Primitive Roots, Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541; Hestenes, D. and Sobczyk, G. (1984) Clifford Algebra to Geometric Calculus, Reidel, ISBN 978-90-277-1673-6. CLAIM: (C1) Six-phase (n=6, step=4): M^0+M^4+M^8+M^12+M^16+M^20 equiv 0 mod 9; three M^12-antipodal pairs each cancel. (C2) Twelve-phase (n=12, step=2): sum of 12 equidistant matrices equiv 0 mod 9; six antipodal pairs cancel. (C3) Universal n-phase theorem: for all n|24 the sum is I (n=1), 3I (n=3), or 0 (all other n); proved by grade-inversion pairing for all even n (M^k+M^(k+12)=M^k(I+M^12)=M^k(I-I)=0, using cert [298] M^12=-I) and direct computation for n=3 (cert [303]); three-phase is the unique non-zero non-trivial case because n=3 is the only odd non-trivial divisor of 24; det(M^8-I) equiv 0 mod 9 (non-invertible, confirming n=3 sum can be non-zero), det(M^4-I) equiv 4 mod 9 (invertible, forcing n=6 sum to zero). (C4) T^4 maps Cosmos->Cosmos; 72 states form exactly 12 disjoint period-6 sextets; T^2 maps Cosmos->Cosmos; 72 states form exactly 6 disjoint period-12 dodecaplets; orbit_size=24/gcd(step,24); cross-check T^8 gives 24 triads (cert [303]). (C5) Observer layer (Theorem NT): six-phase rational cosine sum 1+1/2-1/2-1-1/2+1/2=0 (exact Fraction); all n>=2 observer sums are zero; discrete layer differs (3I for n=3, 0 for even n) confirming Theorem NT boundary. Builds on cert [291] (M^24=I mod 9), cert [296] (det(M)=-1), cert [298] (M^12=-I grade inversion), cert [299] (Cassini), cert [303] (three-phase=3I). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
